entries.summary: OpenAI publicly released ChatGPT, a free conversational chatbot built on GPT-3.5.
Sam Altman said ChatGPT had crossed one million users within five days of launch.
Microsoft confirmed a multi-year, multibillion-dollar new investment expanding its OpenAI partnership.
OpenAI introduced ChatGPT Plus, a twenty-dollar monthly subscription with priority access.
Analysts estimated ChatGPT had reached one hundred million monthly users in January.
Microsoft unveiled an AI-powered Bing search and Edge browser using OpenAI technology.
OpenAI launched GPT-4, a multimodal model accepting both image and text inputs.
OpenAI opened ChatGPT plugins, letting the chatbot browse and use external services.
OpenAI released a free ChatGPT iOS app, expanding mobile access for users.
OpenAI made Code Interpreter generally available to ChatGPT Plus subscribers.
OpenAI added voice conversations and image inputs to ChatGPT for paying users.
At its first DevDay, OpenAI announced GPT-4 Turbo and customizable GPTs.
OpenAI opened the GPT Store, letting Plus users share custom GPT chatbots.
OpenAI previewed Sora, a model generating realistic minute-long videos from text.
OpenAI introduced GPT-4o, a faster multimodal model with real-time voice.
OpenAI released o1-preview, a reasoning model trained to think before responding.
OpenAI launched ChatGPT Pro at two hundred dollars and the full o1 model.
OpenAI previewed o3, a successor reasoning model, in a live-streamed demonstration.
OpenAI made ChatGPT publicly available, sparking widespread interest in generative AI.
Microsoft confirmed a fresh, reportedly ten-billion-dollar investment in San Francisco-based OpenAI.
OpenAI began offering ChatGPT Plus, a paid tier promising faster, more reliable responses.
Microsoft launched a ChatGPT-powered Bing in a direct challenge to Google search.
OpenAI unveiled GPT-4, claiming improved reasoning and the ability to interpret images.
Tech leaders signed an open letter calling for a six-month pause on advanced AI training.
Sam Altman urged the US Senate to regulate large artificial intelligence systems.
ChatGPT became OpenAI's standout product as competitors raced to release rival chatbots.
OpenAI announced ChatGPT could now see, hear, and speak using voice and images.
At DevDay in San Francisco, OpenAI introduced custom GPTs and a cheaper API.
OpenAI launched a marketplace for custom GPT chatbots built by third-party developers.
OpenAI demonstrated Sora, a text-to-video tool that produced unusually realistic short clips.
OpenAI showed GPT-4o, an assistant capable of near-instant spoken back-and-forth conversation.
OpenAI began testing SearchGPT, a search prototype meant to challenge Google directly.
OpenAI released o1, marketed as a reasoning model that pauses to think first.
OpenAI introduced ChatGPT search, integrating live web results into the chatbot.
OpenAI introduced a two-hundred-dollar monthly ChatGPT Pro plan with unlimited o1 access.
